summary: Joel Spolsky announces that the weekly StackOverflow podcasts he does with Jeff Atwood are moving to IT Conversations, a large network of technology audio shows. The post is a brief administrative update providing the new RSS feed URL and instructions for subscribing via iTunes. Joel expresses mild self-deprecation about joining such a well-regarded podcast network.
key_points: The StackOverflow podcast with Jeff Atwood had already produced eight episodes at the time of writing
The podcast was moving to IT Conversations, described as a large network of technology audio shows
Joel uses a self-deprecating analogy comparing himself to a casually dressed person walking into a fine restaurant
money_quotes: Just looking at all the great shows they have there makes me feel a bit like a kid in jeans and a T-shirt with a dirty slogan who just walked into Chez Panisse.
key_insight: A brief logistical announcement about the StackOverflow podcast's move to IT Conversations, notable mainly as a historical marker in the early days of the StackOverflow project.
